name: batch_read
description: Read multiple files or line ranges in one native tool call, with optional cd, head/tail/line-numbering, line counting, grep filtering with max-results, context, find+read, show-all, and open-ended ranges.

parameters.name: files
parameters.description: One or more file paths, optionally with :start-end, :start- (open-ended), or :-end (from start) line ranges

parameters.name: --cd=DIR
parameters.description: Change to DIR before reading files

parameters.name: --tail=N
parameters.description: Show last N lines, or +N to show all from line N

parameters.name: --lines=start-end
parameters.description: Show line range for subsequent files; start- for open-ended

parameters.name: --count-lines
parameters.description: Show line count for each file (like wc -l)

parameters.name: --grep=PATTERN
parameters.description: Show only lines matching extended regex

parameters.name: --max-results=N
parameters.description: Limit grep output to N lines (like grep PATTERN file | head -N); applies after grep filtering

parameters.name: --context=N or -C N
parameters.description: Show N lines of context before and after each grep match (like grep -C)

parameters.name: -A N
parameters.description: Show N lines of context AFTER each grep match (like grep -A)

parameters.name: -B N
parameters.description: Show N lines of context BEFORE each grep match (like grep -B)

parameters.name: --find=GLOB
parameters.description: Find files by glob pattern (uses -name for simple names, -path for patterns with /)

parameters.name: --brief
parameters.description: Suppress file headers and separators for compact output

examples.expected: Shows first 5 matching class definitions with line context
Shows function signatures plus 5 lines of body after each match

cost_saving_rationale: Collapses many sequential cat/head/tail/wc/grep/nl/cat -A calls and cd+read patterns into one step, saving N-1 agent steps; open-ended ranges eliminate tail -n +N pipelines; --find=GLOB replaces find ... -exec cat {} \; patterns; --context/-A/-B replaces cat file | grep -A5/grep -B2/grep -C3 patterns seen in samples, collapsing 2-step cat+grep pipelines into one step per function lookup; --max-results=N eliminates grep PATTERN file | head -N pipelines, collapsing 2-step grep+head patterns into one step.
